feed.title: 8holic
feed.subtitle: The main hub by 8holic (Real name Bryan) to create a hub to link all existing projects and files.

entry.title: Book Reading Reflection
entry.content:
Background

I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below

What type of person do I want to be?

This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead.

What am I doing NOW

-Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room

What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Studying OSINT/tech related things Going to University

I was a little suprise to see that I listed both studying and University. I listed the two and I think I see the issue

Aspect University Education Self Study (OSINT/tech)
Pace Fixed, often rushed Self‑paced, can slow down to understand
Teaching style Professors focused on research, not teaching Self‑selected resources, learn by doing
Motivation Studying for tests, external pressure Internal curiosity, goals I set myself
Goal setting Curriculum‑driven, rigid I set the study goals and path
Schedule control Fixed class times, attendance requirements I decide when, how long, and the rhythm
Autonomy over content Core subjects, prerequisites, limited choice I choose exactly what I want, in any order
Evaluation pressure Grades, exams, GPA, bell curve Self‑assessment, pulbic perception
Hierarchy Professors as authority, power distance Bad resources get discarded
Intellectual freedom Curriculum decides what’s relevant I get to chase after useless knowledge

I did not realize these restriction until I head to University Charles III of Madrid for an exchange semester, where the way lessosn are conducted there affected my thoughts. The key difference is the the professors are more open to questions and meeting with students, as well as more intellectual freedom to explore topics we are interested in(even if my group squandered it). I think this is why it is more toleratable even through it is signficantly more difficult. I want to solve the ‘how’ and not go through a rigid process.

Based on this, here is my first identity: I want to be a student of technology.

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Travel overseas Mindlessly Browse the web
Do things with intent Lose time to Listening to Music
Finish reading my bookshelf Stop buying more books
Help uplift the community Eat unhealth sugarly drinks
Go for Health Checkup  

These are group together because they have a common theme, which is active vs passive consumption. The left side are about doing and experiencing things, while most of the thing I want to stop are passive consumptions. For things on the left I felt that they are usually harder to start, however upon starting it is harder to stop. Whereas things on the right are easy to start and easy to stay engaged, but at the end I felt that I haven’t done anything.

So the second Identity will be: I want to be someone who does and experience.
entry.summary: Background I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below What type of person do I want to be? This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead. What am I doing NOW -Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

entry.title: URECA Project Presentation
entry.content:
Overview

Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.

I felt that the professor was wonderful, it was mostly unsupervised due to his busy schedule. This gave me a lot of opporunity to mostly figure things out by myself and decide on my course of action, with inputs from him during each presentation.

The things I done well is the following: -The ability to think creatively to solve issues -The ability to work consistenly on the project throughout the semester -The ability to present my work to the professor -The conclusion of the project involved it being presented to people in the community center after the above conference, I think it is a good showcase of the work to a wider audiance.

The things I think I can do better: -Clearer communication or more meaningful communiation to the professor, I am unsure if the update aren’t significant enough or he is busy but sometimes I am not sure how to proceed. Usually I can just figure something out, but at the end when it comes to publishing rights I am not sure if I should do it under my account or under the school since the school owns all the project -Preseting a demo, some of my presentation did not work when presenting to the professor and I believe he had some trouble to get it to work properly.

I think that some factors are out of control as I am not very sure about the school proceedings, NTU being a black box does not help either. While I was unable to publish the work, I hope that I have done enough that the Professor will get there eventurally.
entry.summary: Overview Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 6&7
entry.content:
Overview

So I realize I do not have much to write here, so I combined both days

Wakayama

Intent

Hot Spring are kinda what is popular in Japan, so I went to this hot spring resort.

Experience

As I am aware there is no food I bought some stuff before I went. It was an intresting experience as I was given something to wear in the hotel. My room was facing the ocean. I went for the hot spring and they list the composition(in japanese), soaking inside I can feel like something is on my skin. I went twice, once at night and another in the morning.

In the morning I was at the outdoors section where I can look at the nearby highway. After that I had breakfast which was a spread of random meals. I walked around Kada town a bit before departing.

Memory

The Hot Spring certainly feel nice, it is an unique experience. It was relatively isolated since I arrive late/early respectively.

Insight

I need to stay for more days in the future, one day isn’t enough unfortuantely.

Reflection - Overall

All in all it was an enjoyable trip, however the last few days are hampered by poor planning making them less memorable compared to the first few days. I hope to do this again in the future.
entry.summary: Overview So I realize I do not have much to write here, so I combined both days

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 5
entry.content:
Kyoto, Fushimi Inari Shrine

Intent

To be honest I was trying to avoid the crowd, I thought it will be cool since it will be late at night.

Experience

I went down the shrine at 12AM basically, the entire place feels calming initally. However around the Tori gates the atmosphere become very opressive, espeically as I can hear the wild boars and friends. I did not finish the ascend as planned and I dipped out midway through.

Memory

Something about the night turn a simple walk into a scary one. I think it is the lack of people or the darkness, however the number of Tori gate make the experience a surreal one

Insight

I hope to go again in the morning, through I learnt the importance of pulling out. The entire experience was an unique one but I am unsure if my heart can handle this.

Kyoto, Railway Museum and Tower

Intent

I didn’t have any real goal in mind so I was just googling for intresting things.

Experience/Memory

To be honest it is pretty forgettable, the only thing I rembered is that the fog blocked the view. Oh yes and there is a minature section to drive trains.

Insights

Maybe spamming random activites and commiting to it isn’t the best idea if I clearly don’t care about it.

Kyoto, Gear Play

Intent

I saw the advertisement as a “wordless” play, which is something I think I can enjoy even without a translator so I went inside.

Experience

I sat down and we watched a play, the entire play features 5 character and a set. The production is really high and there are flyers and stuff. I took a few home.

Memory

This was the highlight of the kyoto trip suprising, the entire play was something that convey emotion despite not a single word being spoken. They conveyed the sense of creativity and things quite well, as well as using creative stageplay. The emotions conveyed are really strong, I even bought the stageplay and some shirts. Even if it is all in Japanese so I need to get on with learning the language to read it.

Insight

It seems I had some misconception on plays on account of only being exposed to my school terrible plays. In the future I will seek our more theatres to watch and expand my horizon.

Insight-Overall

I learnt the plan is to plan more time. 1 day in Kyoto is insufficient and one of the main issue I ran into is the fact that I am constrained by time, some attractions and museums are not open that day so I wasn’t able to visit.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 4
entry.content:
Ikoma Sanjo Amusement Park

Intent

An amusement park on the hill was something I was curious about, the main drive is curosity.

Experience

I went up via a train, the amusement park is free to visit. Payment is only for specific rides. I went to the flying plane as well as the “White House”. Afterwards I climbed down the hill and walked back to the train.

Memory

The trains are very cute and have a nice design. The flying plane is great because I was able to see both Nara and Osaka from a high overview. Additionally the White house depicted an intresting version of America, the building is tilted by a few degrees. The founding myth was shown as a bridge, then followed by a steam train. Notably the train noise is clearly a vaccum cleaner. At the end, lego was used to depict the moon landing.

Insight

Overall the entire place was really enjoyable despite the low budget vibe. Some of the creative solutions are something to inspire me. In the future I learn how the budget does not determine the quality.

Nara, Deer Park

Intent

The Nara Park are kinda popular and I heard of them before.

Experience

There are indeed, many deers. I bought some deer biscuit but a single deer forcefully ripped it from my fingers. Additionally I visited the nearby shrines, and sat down on a bench and watch the deers just laze around.

Memory

The sheer number of deers is certainly something to remember, through being robbed by a deer was not expected. Sitting on the bench was calming and relaxing.

Insight

I guess I learnt how scary wild animals can be, and also how calming is it to do nothing. I wish to come back to stare at the deers in the future.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 4
entry.content:
Tomogashima Island

Intent

As the weather is great, and I am always curious about abandoned places(since they are illegal to visit in Singapore), I decide to pull the trigger for this. I was expecting a hike basically in the wildness

Experience

It took 1 hour to get from Osaka to Wakayama,kada Village.At 9am where the ferry is suppose to leave the weather suddendly worsen causing the ferry to halt. However at 1030am all is good so I went ahead. On the island I visited the observatory and it is pretty nice. I also met up with two nice lads and we became acquainted. At the end we missed the initial ferry back so we waited together for an hour for the 4pm ferry.

Memory

For a moment I was concern that the trip needs to be cancelled. The intresting things are the world war 2 fort as well as former shophouses in the era. Additionally there was an statue there.

Insight

-I learnt from the other two to have a more exposed field of view, and I find it rather meaningful. Paying attention to the rocks for example he can tell that it is recently exposed because the algae growth is lesser compared to others. -Additionally talking to other random traveller is a risk worth taking. I am usually rather reserved but it was a really enjoyable experience.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 3
entry.content:
Overview

Today the plan was to visit the Osaka Aquarium, the boat ride and Umeda Sky. After the previous packed day I decide to go for a lighter day.

Osaka Aquarium

Intent

So the honest reason was that I haven’t really visit many Aquariums so I was curious. I also saw it in an anime

Experience

The aquarium started with a nice outdoor section,followed by a lot of animals. There are a lot of information avilable for people. I also paid for the feeding area.

Memory

The caretakers are very knowledgeble and speak some english. The following are some intresting things I learnt -The animals do not attack each other when well fed which is how they kept everyone in the same tank.

Insight

I learn some intresting things about animal, from this I learnt to give unconventional experience a go. Even through I am not a marine entuhaist I still learnt some intresting facts and understand the animals better.

Umeda Sky and Car Museum

Intent

For Umeda Sky I thought that the skyline would be nice since I have seen the older side I am curious how does the more modern side look. I happen to stumble across the Car Museum so I payed it a visit

Experience

Upon reaching the top I see two side, one side is the river facing side and the other side is mor eurban The Car Museum is a small but neat museum and features cars from the start of car development to current cars.

Memory

For both they aren’t a lot of intresting details but the sights is nice.

Insight

Sometimes a slow relaxing day is fine, to simply relax and look around.
entry.summary: Overview Today the plan was to visit the Osaka Aquarium, the boat ride and Umeda Sky. After the previous packed day I decide to go for a lighter day.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 2
entry.content:
Overview

Today was packed with two piority, I visited the Osaka Castle as well as the World Expo.

Osaka Castle

Intent

I was interested in old stuff, so I expect to see an old castle preserved into the modern era and maybe learn some history about it.

Experience

The Anime Figurine Museum was a suprise which was actually the first thing I saw. As it was part of my ticket I went in and saw figures across the centuries. After buying some snacks from here, I proceed to the castle and found that it is now a museum basically. I also took a boat ride around the moat of the castle.

Memory

Regarding the Anime Figurine figure it was intresting to look at the progression of the figurine across time and how it has changed. Additionally there are diorama of various famous scene like Iron Man and the fighter jet as well as Godzilla. For the castle it was more modern than expected, the interior feels like any other museum. It is english translated and some things are intresting, such as a particular section on Dutch Japan friendship where I learnt that the Dutch is one of the few that is trading with Japan during the isolation. Finally the boat ride I remember seeing the “demon face” statue, where the guide told us that it is suppose to ward off evil, I thought this was intresting to witness thses superstition.

Insight

The most important lesson from this is definetly time management, I did not research everything and I missed a boat ride as well as one of the tower due to taking a wrong turn. In the future I will either allocate more time or at least look out for time sensitive parts in a region.

Japan World Expo 2025

Intent - Overall

The mainstay of the trip, my professor mentioned this event and showed us an video. Since then I have been kinda curious about this event which started back in 1897, and it seems like a good way to learn much about the world. I booked the night ticket expecting it to be less crowded. I have also receive a slot at the Japanese Pavilion via the lottery system. I will be splitting this section sequentially based on my arrival

Experience - Malaysia & Singapore Pavilion

The Malaysia building represent a wooden strucutre and the first part is a market. Afterwards there are some sections on the hope of the nation and their smart city plan. Singapore building is a giant red dot that certainly stand out, the first floor is full of glass trees and simillar things,

Memory - Malaysia & Singapore Pavilion

Malaysia section most memorial section is the entrance where it is about past culture, everything after that feel like generic big city stuff. Singapore memorial section is the glass section on the first floor, there was one section using Augmented Reality where we can enter our wish and hopes

Insight - Malaysia & Singapore Pavilion

I learnt not to visit surrounding country as the information isn’t new and while visually it is appealing, the content are mostly of things I already know.

Experience - Japan Pavillion

The Japan Pavllion actually consist of three sections represnting a cyclic cycle for a substainable future. It was also as I learn the largest wooden strucutre at that time. I saw a showcase of how biodegrabale plastic break down in water exhibit, as well as a comic strip of doeramon of the Ise Jingu. Finally we reach the center 1/3 of the way through and there is a queue for looking at the Narashino Meteorite and one for touching it, obviously I went and touch it and it feel just like any other rock. Afterwards it is focused on the bio reactor and how they produce energy with algea, ending off with DNA strands like decorations with mirrors making it appear like the DNA strands in a human body.

Memory - Japan Pavillion

There are a lot of intresting things here, so to summarise

  • The Ise Jingu and the ceremonic rebuilding every 20 years to ensure that Shinmei-zukuri technique used is preserved across generation as well as the structure of the building and it clearly seems to be working pretty well.
  • A showcase of how much more water does it take to raise an animal compared to algea, the ration is 50:1. It is hard to imagine how much meat consumption affect the planet.
  • The central ring was really relaxing and calming, a good waiting spot for the Narashino Meteorite. Even through the entire day was quited packed this moment was one of calm.
  • The bio reactor plant where waste water from the expo is channeled right here and is used to create energy. It took a while to understand but once I realize what is it below my feet it was like a miracle.

Insight - Japan Pavillion

This was one of the best part of the day, the exhibits are exciting. I learnt to stop and contemplate the various exhibitons and stay longer than a glance.

Experience - Conclusion

Initally I had planned to visit other pavilion like China and Spain, however I soon realize that the night ticket means that by the time I have left Japan Pavillion most other Pavillion are starting to close their doors. Some of those that are still open are the Brazil Pavilion as well as Bangledesh. Since we are near the end I went to the top and had an overview of the entire place and watched the final drone show before heading back. The queue back is long but the Japanese train were efficient

Memory - Others

The Brazil pavilion left the most impact due to being a shade of pink with plastic represnting trees,where the tree seems to grow and inflate. I sat at one of the chair and it was a strange experience. The train back is also very long, I think it took 50 minute for me to leave via the station.

Insight - Others

One of the things I learnt is to trust my instints and explore what I am curious about. Additionally I need to do more research before buying tickets as I did not get to visit as many pavilion as I wanted. FInally I learnt how to tolerate long queues.
entry.summary: Overview Today was packed with two piority, I visited the Osaka Castle as well as the World Expo.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 1
entry.content:
Overview

Today is the first day I arrive in Japan. I took the plane in midnight and arrive in the morning. Before heading to my hostel I visited the following location.

Cup Noodles Museum Osaka Ikeda

Intent

As a college student who eat a lot of instant noodles to survive for my college exam, I am very famillar with Japanese branded instant noodle. The Cheesy Currry Chicken is my favorite. So when I was looking around and see a Cup Noodles Museum I decide to visit just to see what is up.

Experience

The museum is smaller than expected, as most of the things are in Japanse I did not really understood much of the exhibit. However the making of the instant noodles is worth it.

Memory

The Instant Noodle making is the highlight, I get to design my own cup and add my own ingreidents. Additionally I learnt how are instant noodles are prepared. I get to see the lid sealer that melts the lid onto the cup.

Insight

For small and niche museums such as these the piority is the interactivity and not the exhibits itself. I should look up such activites in the future before visiting.

Ikeda Castle Ruins

Intent

It happen to be nearby, and I kinda like castle. So I decide to visit it since it is convenient.

Experience

There is a park around the castle, at the top floor it leads to an observatory that provide a overview of the more civilian part of the city. The castle has a Stair climbing wheelchair, additionally there are a few small gardens.

Memory

The sunset is a nice sight(As seen in the picture above). It become very quiet the instant I enter the garden, having quiet and peace is nice. Additionally thet Stair Climbing Wheelchair atttached to this, made me realize how rare this is in my home country.

Insight

Sponatenous trip that is effortless to visit are worth the efforts, no need to plan rigidly for everything. Additionally I realize the gap in infrastrucutre in terms of disability access, and this is something worth advocating.

Dotonbori

Intent

It is suprising close to the hostel I was staying at so I paid it a visit. I also heard something about the running man.

Experience

It is really crowded, and yup the running man is there. I ate some nice food and walk around a bit.

Memory

The food is nice, also I saw Spiderman on the bridge.

Insight

I realize that I should probably research more about the famous landmarks, since without any knowledge of the historical significance the running man is just another banner.
entry.summary: Overview Today is the first day I arrive in Japan. I took the plane in midnight and arrive in the morning. Before heading to my hostel I visited the following location.
